__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_0__: MRR vs Revenue Quality: Why Headline MRR Misleads Buyers | Churn Lens
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_16__: $50K MRR sounds great — until you learn 15% is zombie revenue, 30% is concentrated in three customers, and 40% is on annual plans renewing next quarter. Here's how to separate vanity MRR from durable revenue.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_17__: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) is the headline number every SaaS seller quotes. It's also the number most likely to mislead a buyer. MRR tells you how much revenue is billed this month — it says nothing about how much of that revenue will still exist in 6, 12, or 18 months. Revenue quality is the answer to that question.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_18__: Why MRR Is a Vanity Metric
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_19__: MRR counts every active subscription at its current billing rate. It makes no distinction between:
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_1__: Raw MRR is a vanity metric. Learn what revenue quality actually means — net revenue retention, expansion sustainability, concentration adjustment, and durable MRR.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_20__: A customer who logged in today and is actively expanding their usage
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_21__: A customer who hasn't opened the product in 90 days but hasn't cancelled yet
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_22__: A customer who represents 25% of total MRR and could leave at any time
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_23__: A customer on a legacy discounted plan paying 60% less than market rate
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_24__: All four show up identically in the MRR number. But they represent vastly different levels of revenue durability.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_26__: Revenue quality is a composite assessment of how durable and trustworthy the MRR number is. It accounts for five factors:
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_28__: What percentage of MRR from period N is still present in period N+12, excluding expansion? This is the floor — the worst-case retention scenario. Healthy SaaS: 85-95%.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_2__: MRR vs Revenue Quality: Why Headline MRR Misleads Buyers
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_30__: Same as GRR but including expansion revenue. Above 100% means existing customers are growing faster than churn. This is the gold standard metric.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_32__: GRR/NRR adjusted for customer concentration risk. If your top customer represents 30% of MRR, effective retention is lower because their departure has outsized impact.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_34__: MRR weighted by product engagement levels. Inactive accounts (
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_38__: Is expansion coming from genuine upsell (more seats, higher tier) or from price increases that customers may resist at renewal?
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_39__: Net Revenue Retention: The Metric That Matters
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_3__: Why raw MRR is a vanity metric and how to calculate true revenue quality for SaaS acquisitions.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_40__: NRR is the single best summary metric for revenue quality. It measures what happened to the revenue from customers you had 12 months ago:
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_41__: NRR = (Starting MRR + Expansion MRR − Churned MRR − Contraction MRR) / Starting MRR
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_4__: MRR vs Revenue Quality: Why Headline MRR Misleads Buyers
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_5__: Why raw MRR is a vanity metric and how to calculate true revenue quality for SaaS acquisitions.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_61__: Upload the target's subscription data and Churn Lens automatically computes GRR, NRR, concentration-adjusted retention, engagement-weighted MRR, and a composite Revenue Quality Score. See the true revenue picture in minutes.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_64__: Expansion revenue (upsells, seat additions, plan upgrades) can make NRR look great while masking underlying problems. Watch for:
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_66__: Revenue grows because prices increased, not because customers gained value. This is unsustainable — eventually customers churn due to price.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_68__: A large enterprise customer keeps adding seats — but those seats are unused. This is just delayed concentration risk.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_70__: Customers upgraded because old tiers were deprecated. They may not renew at the new price.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_71__: Genuine expansion comes from customers getting more value and naturally scaling their usage. Check whether expansion correlates with engagement growth — if MRR is rising but login frequency isn't, the expansion may be artificial.
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_72__: Durable MRR: The Number You Should Value
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_73__: After adjusting for all quality factors, you arrive at
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_75__: — the revenue you can reasonably expect to persist for 12+ months post-acquisition:
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_76__: Durable MRR = Total MRR − Zombie MRR − Concentration Risk Buffer − Annual Cliff Exposure
__TR_mrr-vs-revenue-quality_77__: This is the number that should drive your acquisition valuation. It's typically 70-90% of headline MRR for healthy SaaS, and can be as low as 50-60% for problematic targets. For the full scoring methodology, see our
